← All Digests Monday, March 16, 2026

"Brand Twins" & A2A Commerce Arrive, Microsoft Copilot Hits 15M Seats, Shadow AI Crisis Deepens

Today's top stories in AI + Product Marketing

1

"Brand Twins" and Agent-to-Agent Commerce Are Here

One of 2026's most fascinating disruptions: consumers' AI agents now negotiate directly with brands' AI agents to get the best prices, promotions, and terms. Marketers must now convince both humans through emotion and AI through logic. The battle for attention just got a new front.

Read on JB Impact →
2

Microsoft Copilot Hits 15 Million Paid Seats

Copilot grew 160% YoY, with enterprise deployments of 35K+ seats tripling. Daily active users up 10x. Microsoft just announced a new E7 tier at $99/user/month (65% higher than E5) including Copilot AI, security, and governance tools. Also: 80% of Fortune 500 now have active AI agents built with Copilot Studio.

Read on Motley Fool →
3

Microsoft: From Tools to "Thinking Systems"

Microsoft's 2026 AI vision centers on systems with persistent memory that remember your preferences, workflows, and context across sessions. The shift: from reactive tools you call once to proactive partners that anticipate needs. This is what they call "the invisible but fundamental turning point."

Read on Windows News →
4

Accel + Google Fund 5 AI Startups (From 4,000 Applicants)

The 2026 Atoms AI Cohort was just announced: K-Dense (AI co-scientist), Dodge.ai (autonomous ERP agents), Persistence Labs (voice AI for call centers), Zingroll (AI-native streaming), and LevelPlane (industrial automation). Each gets $2M + $350K in Google Cloud/Gemini/DeepMind credits.

Read on Rediff →
5

The "Shadow AI" Crisis: 78% Using Their Own Tools

While 91% of marketers use AI, adoption is happening massively bottom-up. According to the Work Trend Index, 78% of employees use their own AI tools without governance (BYOAI). This "Shadow AI" is now a security and brand consistency vulnerability. The experiment phase is over — organizations that don't structure this are losing to faster competitors.

Read on JB Impact →

💡 My Take

The Brand Twins piece is a must-read. When consumer AI agents start filtering which brands even get through to their humans, the game changes completely. Positioning and differentiation matter more than ever — not just for human perception, but for machine logic. If your brand can't pass the AI gatekeeper test, the human never sees you.
